   - Some organizations restrict email forwarding for security reasons. Check with your IT department if you encounter issues.

2. **External Domains**
   - Forwarding to non-company email addresses (e.g., personal Gmail or Yahoo accounts) may be blocked. Ensure your forwarding address is within allowed domains.

3. **Spam Filters**
   - If the forwarding address is flagged as suspicious or blacklisted, emails may not reach it. Contact IT support to resolve any filtering issues.

4. **Account Settings**
   - Ensure that your account settings are configured correctly. Missing settings or misconfigurations can prevent forwarding.

5. **Quota Limits**
   - Exceeding mail storage limits can disrupt email forwarding. Check your account storage and clear out unnecessary emails if needed.

6. **System Outages**
   - Temporary issues with your email provider may impact forwarding functionality. Check the service status page or reach out to support if you suspect an outage.

By following these steps and being aware of potential blocks, you can effectively set up and manage email forwarding within your organization. For further assistance, please contact the IT support team.

title: Scheduling a Teams meeting
body:
# Scheduling a Teams Meeting

**Category:** Email

To schedule a Teams meeting, follow the steps outlined below. These instructions apply to both the Microsoft Teams desktop application and the web version.

### Steps to Schedule a Teams Meeting

1. **Open Microsoft Teams:**
   - Launch the Microsoft Teams application on your desktop or navigate to [teams.microsoft.com](https://teams.microsoft.com) in your web browser.

2. **Go to the Calendar:**
   - In the left-hand sidebar, click on the **Calendar** icon. This will display your calendar along with any existing meetings.

3. **Select New Meeting:**
   - In the upper right corner of the Calendar view, click on the **New Meeting** button. This will open a scheduling form.

4. **Fill Out Meeting Details:**
   - **Title:** Enter a clear and concise title for the meeting.
   - **Attendees:** In the "Invite people" field, add the email addresses of the participants you wish to invite. You can also search for names from your organization’s directory.
   - **Date and Time:** Choose the date and start/end times for your meeting. You can also select the "All day" option if it’s a full-day event.
   - **Location:** If the meeting is in-person, specify the location. For a Teams meeting, you can leave this field blank or type "Microsoft Teams Meeting."
   - **Details:** Use the large text box to provide any additional information or agenda items for the meeting.

5. **Set Meeting Options (Optional):**
   - Click on the **Meeting options** link at the bottom of the form to customize settings such as who can present and if attendees can bypass the lobby. Adjust these settings according to your needs.

6. **Save the Meeting:**
   - Once all details are filled in, click the **Save** button located at the bottom right of the form. This will send an invitation to all participants and add the meeting to your calendar.

7. **Check Calendar Confirmation:**
   - After saving, verify that the meeting appears in your calendar. If necessary, you can click on the meeting entry to view details or make adjustments.

8. **Send a Reminder (Optional):**
   - To ensure attendees remember the meeting, consider sending a reminder via email or a Teams message a day before the meeting.

### Tips:

- **Recurring Meetings:** If this is a recurring meeting, select the "Does not repeat" dropdown to choose a recurrence pattern (daily, weekly, monthly, etc.) before saving.
  
- **Join the Meeting:** On the day of the meeting, participants can join directly from their calendar by clicking on the meeting entry and selecting "Join."

By following these steps, you can efficiently schedule a Teams meeting and ensure all participants are informed and prepared.

title: Setting up email via the legacy OWA portal
body:
# Setting Up Email via the Legacy OWA Portal

**Category:** Email

This article provides step-by-step instructions for setting up your email account using the legacy Outlook Web App (OWA) portal. Follow these practical steps to access your email effectively.

## Steps to Set Up Email via Legacy OWA

1. **Open Your Web Browser**
   - Launch any web browser (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Edge).

2. **Navigate to the OWA Portal**
   - In the address bar, type in the URL: `https://legacymail.yourcompany.com/owa` and press `Enter`.

3. **Enter Your Credentials**
   - On the login page, input your company email address (e.g., johndoe@yourcompany.com).
   - Enter your password in the corresponding field.
   - Click the `Sign In` button.

4. **Accept Security Certificate (if prompted)**
   - If you see a security warning regarding the site’s certificate, click on `Advanced`, then select `Proceed to legacymail.yourcompany.com (unsafe)`. (Note: This is common for legacy portals and should only be done if you are certain of the site’s legitimacy.)

5. **Configure Your Inbox Settings**
   - Once logged in, you will be directed to your inbox.
   - Click on the gear icon located in the top-right corner.
   - Select `Options` from the dropdown menu.

6. **Set Up Your Email Preferences**
   - Under `Mail`, you can adjust settings such as:
     - **Automatic Replies**: Configure out-of-office messages.
     - **Signature**: Create or modify your email signature.
     - **Forwarding**: Set up email forwarding if needed.

7. **Check Mailbox Folders**
   - Ensure that your mailbox folders (Inbox, Sent Items, Drafts, etc.) are visible in the left sidebar.
   - If folders are missing, click on `Refresh` to reload the mailbox.

8. **Compose a Test Email**
   - Click on `New` to compose a new email.
   - Enter your own email address in the `To` field, add a subject, and write a brief message.
   - Click `Send` to send the test email.
   - Check your inbox to confirm receipt of the test email.

9. **Log Out Securely**
   - Once you have finished setting up your email, click on your profile picture or initials in the top-right corner.
   - Select `Sign Out` to log out of the OWA portal securely.

10. **Contact IT Support (if needed)**
    - If you encounter any issues during setup, please reach out to IT Support at it-support@yourcompany.com or call the helpdesk at extension 1234.

## Additional Tips
- Make sure your browser is updated to ensure compatibility with the legacy OWA portal.
- For security, avoid sharing your login credentials with anyone.
- Bookmark the OWA URL for easy access in the future.

By following these steps, you should be able to successfully set up and access your email via the legacy OWA portal.

title: Understanding email storage quotas
body:
# Understanding Email Storage Quotas

## Category: Email

As part of our ongoing efforts to maintain an efficient email system, it’s important to understand how email storage quotas work and how to manage your mailbox effectively. This article outlines the key aspects of email storage quotas and provides practical steps to help you stay within your limit.

### What is an Email Storage Quota?

An email storage quota is the maximum amount of data that you can store in your email account. This quota includes all your emails, attachments, and any other files stored within your mailbox. When you reach your quota limit, you will be unable to send or receive any new emails until you reduce your storage usage.

### Checking Your Email Storage Quota

1. **Log in to your email account.**
   - Use your corporate email credentials to access your mailbox.

2. **Navigate to the settings.**
   - Look for a gear icon or a settings menu in your email interface.

3. **Locate storage information.**
   - Find the section labeled “Storage,” “Account,” or “Usage” to see your current quota and how much space you have used.

### Managing Your Email Storage

To avoid hitting your email storage quota, follow these practical steps:

1. **Delete Unnecessary Emails:**
   - Regularly review your inbox, sent items, and other folders.
   - Delete emails that are no longer needed, especially those with large attachments.

2. **Use the Search Function:**
   - Utilize the search bar to find and delete specific types of emails, such as those containing large attachments.
   - Example: Search for emails with attachments by using keywords like “has:attachment”.

3. **Archive Old Emails:**
   - Move older emails to an archive folder if they need to be retained but are not frequently accessed.
   - Check if your email system supports archiving features.

4. **Manage Attachments:**
   - Save important attachments to a shared drive or cloud storage instead of keeping them in your email.
   - Delete the original email after saving the attachment.

5. **Organize with Folders:**
   - Create folders to categorize your emails. This helps in easily identifying and managing emails that you can delete or archive.

6. **Regular Maintenance:**
   - Set a reminder to review and clean your mailbox at least once a month to keep your storage usage in check.

### What to Do If You Reach Your Quota

If you reach your email storage quota:

1. **Delete or Archive Emails:**
   - Follow the steps above to clear out unnecessary emails or archive old ones.

2. **Contact IT Support:**
   - If you need additional help managing your storage or if you believe there’s an error in your storage calculation, reach out to the IT support team.

3. **Consider Upgrading:**
   - If your role requires a significant amount of email storage, discuss potential options for an increased quota with your manager or IT.

### Conclusion

Understanding and managing your email storage quota is essential for maintaining effective communication within the organization. By following these practical steps, you can ensure that you stay within your limits and avoid disruptions to your email service. Regular maintenance and organization will help keep your mailbox manageable and efficient.

title: Managing a delegated mailbox
body:
# Managing a Delegated Mailbox

**Category:** Email

Delegated mailboxes are useful for teams or individuals who need to share email responsibilities. This guide outlines the steps to effectively manage a delegated mailbox in our corporate email system.

## Steps to Manage a Delegated Mailbox

### 1. Access the Delegated Mailbox
   - Open your email client (Outlook, webmail, etc.).
   - In Outlook, click on your profile name in the top right corner.
   - Select “Open another mailbox” from the dropdown menu.
   - Enter the email address of the delegated mailbox and click “Open”.

### 2. Reviewing Emails
   - Once the delegated mailbox is open, navigate to the "Inbox."
   - Review unread emails, paying attention to priority and time-sensitive messages.
   - Use the sorting and filtering features to categorize emails by date, sender, or subject.

### 3. Responding to Emails
   - To reply to an email, click on the message to open it.
   - Click on the “Reply” or “Reply All” button.
   - Ensure that your response is appropriate for the delegation context, mentioning your name if necessary to clarify who is responding.

### 4. Forwarding Emails
   - If an email needs to be forwarded, select the email and click the “Forward” button.
   - Add the recipient’s email address and include any necessary context or notes.
   - Click “Send” to forward the email.

### 5. Organizing Emails
   - Create folders in the delegated mailbox for better organization (e.g., “Urgent,” “Follow Up,” “Completed”).
   - Move emails into the appropriate folders by dragging and dropping or using the “Move” option.
   - Consider setting up rules to automatically sort incoming emails based on specific criteria.

### 6. Managing Calendar Events
   - If the delegated mailbox includes a calendar, access it by clicking on the “Calendar” icon.
   - Review upcoming events and appointments.
   - To create a new event, click on “New Event,” fill in the details, and invite attendees as necessary.

### 7. Setting Up Automatic Replies
   - If you anticipate being away from the delegated mailbox, set up an automatic reply.
   - Go to the settings of the delegated mailbox, find “Automatic Replies,” and enable it.
   - Write a clear message specifying your absence and alternative contact information.

### 8. Leaving Notes for Team Members
   - Use the “Notes” feature (if available) to leave messages for team members who may also manage the mailbox.
   - Be concise and clear about the status of ongoing tasks or emails requiring attention.

### 9. Logging Out
   - After managing the mailbox, ensure you log out properly.
   - Close the delegated mailbox by selecting your profile name and choosing “Close.”
   - Log out of your email client if using a public or shared computer for security reasons.

## Tips for Effective Management
- Regularly check the delegated mailbox to stay on top of emails and tasks.
- Maintain clear communication with the primary mailbox owner to ensure alignment on priorities.
- Document any critical actions taken within the mailbox for future reference.

By following these steps, you can efficiently manage a delegated mailbox and enhance collaboration within your team.

title: Software reimbursement policy
body:
# Software Reimbursement Policy

## Category: Other

At [Company Name], we understand that employees may need to purchase software to enhance their productivity and effectiveness in their roles. This article outlines the steps required to request reimbursement for software purchases made for work-related purposes.

### Eligibility

Before submitting a reimbursement request, please ensure that the software meets the following criteria:

1. The software must be directly related to your job responsibilities.
2. The software must not be available through existing company resources or licenses.
3. The cost of the software should not exceed $500 without prior approval.
4. Purchases must be made within the current fiscal year.

### Steps to Request Software Reimbursement

1. **Purchase the Software**
   - Make the purchase using a personal payment method (credit/debit card, etc.).
   - Keep the receipt or confirmation email, as it will be required for reimbursement.

2. **Complete the Reimbursement Form**
   - Access the reimbursement form from the [Company Intranet/SharePoint/Link to Form].
   - Fill out all required fields, including:
     - Employee Name
     - Employee ID
     - Department
     - Purchase Date
     - Software Name
     - Description of how the software will be used for work
     - Total Amount Paid

3. **Attach Supporting Documents**
   - Upload a digital copy of the receipt or invoice.
   - If applicable, provide any additional documentation that supports the necessity of the software (e.g., emails, project descriptions).

4. **Submit for Approval**
   - Review your completed form and attached documents carefully.
   - Submit the form through the designated process (e.g., via email to your manager or through the online reimbursement system).

5. **Await Approval**
   - Your manager will review the reimbursement request. If approved, it will be forwarded to the finance department for processing.
   - If denied, you will receive feedback explaining the reason for the decision.

6. **Receive Reimbursement**
   - Approved reimbursements will be processed in the next payroll cycle.
   - You will receive a notification once the reimbursement is completed.

### Important Notes

- Reimbursement requests must be submitted within 30 days of purchase.
- Ensure that any software purchased complies with company policies regarding security and licensing.
- For software purchases over $500, prior written approval from your department head is required before making the purchase.

### Contact Information

For any questions or further clarification regarding the software reimbursement policy, please contact the IT Support Team at [IT Support Email] or HR at [HR Email].

title: Hardware refresh policy
body:
# Hardware Refresh Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the guidelines and procedures for the regular refresh of hardware within the organization to ensure optimal performance, security, and support.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees who utilize company-provided hardware, including desktops, laptops, and peripherals.

### Policy Overview
1. **Hardware Lifecycle**: All hardware will be evaluated every three years for replacement or upgrade.
2. **Eligibility**: Hardware eligible for refresh includes all company-owned devices that are over three years old or are experiencing performance issues.
3. **Budgeting**: A budget for hardware refresh will be allocated annually, based on departmental needs and inventory assessments.

### Refresh Process

1. **Assessment of Current Hardware**
   - Each department manager will conduct a quarterly assessment of all hardware in use.
   - Identify devices that are three years or older, or those that are experiencing significant performance issues.

2. **Request for Refresh**
   - Complete the Hardware Refresh Request Form available on the intranet.
   - Submit the form to your department manager for approval.

3. **Review and Approval**
   - Department managers will review requests and prioritize them based on necessity and budget constraints.
   - Approved requests will be forwarded to the IT department for processing.

4. **Procurement of New Hardware**
   - The IT department will coordinate with the procurement team to acquire the necessary hardware.
   - Ensure that new hardware meets company specifications and security requirements.

5. **Deployment of New Hardware**
   - Schedule a deployment date with the affected employees.
   - IT staff will assist in migrating data from old hardware to new devices, ensuring minimal disruption to workflow.

6. **Decommissioning Old Hardware**
   - Once the new hardware is deployed, old devices will be collected by the IT department.
   - Old hardware will be securely wiped of all data and either refurbished or disposed of in accordance with environmental regulations.

7. **Documentation and Feedback**
   - Document the refresh process, including hardware specifications, costs, and any issues encountered.
   - Collect feedback from employees regarding the new hardware's performance and functionality for continuous improvement.

### Exceptions
- Any exceptions to this policy must be documented and approved by both the department manager and the IT director.
- Urgent hardware needs that arise outside the refresh cycle can be addressed on a case-by-case basis.

### Conclusion
Regular hardware refreshes are essential for maintaining productivity and security within the organization. Following this policy will help ensure that all employees have access to reliable and efficient hardware.

title: Acceptable use policy
body:
# Acceptable Use Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the acceptable use of corporate technology resources to protect the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of our information systems and data.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party users who access corporate technology resources.

### Acceptable Use Guidelines

1. **Authorized Access Only**
   - Use corporate technology resources only for authorized purposes.
   - Do not access, modify, or delete data or systems without appropriate permission.

2. **Respect Privacy**
   - Do not access or share another user’s files, emails, or personal information without explicit consent.
   - Be aware that corporate communications may be monitored.

3. **Data Protection**
   - Store sensitive data only on approved devices and platforms.
   - Encrypt sensitive data when transmitting over networks.

4. **Internet and Software Use**
   - Use the internet responsibly; avoid accessing inappropriate content (e.g., adult material, gambling sites).
   - Install only approved software and applications. Do not download pirated or unverified software.

5. **Email Etiquette**
   - Use a professional tone and language in all corporate communications.
   - Do not use corporate email for personal business or non-work-related activities.

6. **Device Security**
   - Lock your computer screen when away from your desk.
   - Report lost or stolen devices immediately to IT.

7. **Password Management**
   - Use strong, unique passwords for all corporate accounts.
   - Change passwords regularly and do not share them with others.

8. **Social Media Guidelines**
   - Be cautious when discussing company matters on social media; maintain confidentiality.
   - Do not represent the company publicly without authorization.

9. **Remote Work Policies**
   - Follow the same security protocols when working remotely as when in the office.
   - Use a secure VPN connection to access corporate resources.

10. **Incident Reporting**
    - Report any security incidents or policy violations to the IT department immediately.
    - Cooperate with investigations regarding potential breaches of this policy.

### Consequences of Violations
Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment. Legal action may also be pursued in cases of serious violations.

### Acknowledgment
All users must acknowledge their understanding and acceptance of this policy. Please sign and return the acknowledgment form to your manager.

### Review and Updates
This policy will be reviewed annually and updated as necessary to ensure compliance with legal and technological developments. 

For questions or clarifications regarding this policy, please contact the IT department.

title: Bring-your-own-device (BYOD) policy
body:
# Bring-your-own-device (BYOD) Policy

## Category: Other

### Overview

This article outlines the Bring-your-own-device (BYOD) policy for employees at [Company Name]. This policy allows employees to use their personal devices for work-related activities while ensuring security and compliance with company standards.

### Policy Guidelines

1. **Eligibility**
   - Employees must obtain approval from their manager before using personal devices for work.
   - Devices must be compatible with company systems and meet security requirements.

2. **Device Registration**
   - Employees must register their personal devices with the IT department.
   - Submit a request via the IT Service Desk with the following information:
     - Device type (e.g., smartphone, tablet, laptop)
     - Operating system version
     - Serial number

3. **Security Requirements**
   - All personal devices must have up-to-date antivirus and anti-malware software installed.
   - Devices must be secured with a strong password or biometric authentication.
   - Enable remote wipe capabilities to erase company data in case of loss or theft.

4. **Data Access and Storage**
   - Employees must use company-approved applications for accessing and storing work-related data.
   - Personal cloud storage services (e.g., Google Drive, Dropbox) are not permitted for storing company data.
   - Ensure that sensitive information is not stored locally on personal devices unless explicitly authorized.

5. **Network Access**
   - Personal devices must connect to the company’s secure Wi-Fi network.
   - Use of public Wi-Fi for work-related activities is discouraged; if necessary, use a VPN.

6. **Compliance and Monitoring**
   - Employees must comply with all company policies regarding data protection and confidentiality.
   - The IT department reserves the right to monitor devices for compliance and security purposes.

7. **Support and Maintenance**
   - IT support for personal devices is limited to connectivity and security issues related to company applications.
   - Employees are responsible for maintaining their devices and ensuring they are in good working order.

8. **Termination of BYOD Privileges**
   - BYOD privileges may be revoked if an employee violates the policy or poses a security risk.
   - Upon termination of employment, employees must remove all company data from their personal devices.

### Conclusion

The BYOD policy is designed to enhance flexibility and productivity while ensuring the security of company data. Employees are encouraged to reach out to the IT department for any questions or clarifications regarding this policy.

### Contact Information

For assistance or to report any issues, please contact the IT Service Desk at [IT Service Desk Email] or [IT Service Desk Phone Number].

title: Data classification policy
body:
# Data Classification Policy

## Purpose
The purpose of this policy is to outline the standards for classifying data within the organization to ensure appropriate handling, storage, and sharing practices that protect sensitive information.

## Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who create, access, or manage company data.

## Data Classification Levels
1. **Public**: Information that can be freely shared with anyone. Examples include marketing materials and press releases.
2. **Internal**: Information intended for use within the organization. Examples include internal policies and procedures.
3. **Confidential**: Sensitive information requiring protection from unauthorized access. Examples include employee records and financial data.
4. **Restricted**: Highly sensitive information that requires strict access controls. Examples include trade secrets and proprietary research.

## Data Classification Steps

1. **Identify Data**  
   Conduct an inventory of all data assets within your department. Consider documents, databases, emails, and any other forms of data.

2. **Assess Data Sensitivity**  
   For each data asset, evaluate the sensitivity based on the following criteria:
   - Impact on the organization if data is disclosed, altered, or destroyed.
   - Legal or regulatory requirements for data protection.
   - Stakeholder expectations regarding confidentiality.

3. **Assign Classification Level**  
   Based on the assessment, assign a classification level (Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted) to each data asset. Use the guidelines above to determine the appropriate level.

4. **Label Data**  
   Clearly label all data according to its classification level. Use color coding, watermarks, or metadata tagging to ensure that the classification is visible and easily identifiable.

5. **Implement Access Controls**  
   Establish access controls based on classification levels:
   - **Public**: No restrictions.
   - **Internal**: Accessible to all employees.
   - **Confidential**: Access limited to specific roles or departments.
   - **Restricted**: Access granted on a need-to-know basis, requiring manager approval.

6. **Train Employees**  
   Conduct training sessions to educate employees about the data classification policy, the importance of data protection, and their responsibilities in handling classified data.

7. **Review and Update**  
   Regularly review and update data classifications and access controls to ensure they remain relevant. Perform annual audits of data assets to verify compliance with the classification policy.

8. **Report Incidents**  
   Establish a procedure for reporting data breaches or unauthorized access. Ensure all employees understand how to report incidents promptly to minimize potential damage.

## Conclusion
Adhering to the data classification policy is crucial for safeguarding the organization's information assets. By following these steps, employees can help ensure that data is handled appropriately and securely. For any questions or further clarification, please contact the IT department.

title: Password and authentication policy
body:
# Password and Authentication Policy

## Overview
This document outlines the password and authentication standards for all employees to ensure the security and integrity of corporate systems and data.

## Password Creation Guidelines

1. **Length and Complexity**  
   - Passwords must be at least 12 characters long.
   - Must include a combination of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters (e.g., !@#$%^&*).

2. **Avoid Common Passwords**  
   - Refrain from using easily guessable passwords (e.g., "password," "123456," or personal information like birthdays).
   - Use a password manager to generate and store complex passwords.

3. **Unique Passwords**  
   - Each account must have a unique password.
   - Do not reuse passwords across different systems or applications.

## Password Management

4. **Regular Changes**  
   - Passwords must be changed every 90 days.
   - Users will receive reminders 14 days prior to the expiration of their passwords.

5. **Account Lockout**  
   - After 5 unsuccessful login attempts, the account will be temporarily locked for 30 minutes.
   - If you are locked out, please contact the IT Help Desk for assistance.

## Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

6. **Implementation**  
   - MFA is required for accessing sensitive systems and data.
   - Acceptable forms of MFA include:
     - A one-time code sent to a registered mobile device.
     - Biometric authentication (fingerprint or facial recognition).
     - Hardware tokens.

7. **MFA Enrollment**  
   - Employees must enroll in MFA during their initial login.
   - Ensure your mobile device is registered to receive authentication codes.

## Security Best Practices

8. **Keep Passwords Confidential**  
   - Do not share passwords with anyone, including colleagues and supervisors.
   - Avoid writing down passwords in easily accessible locations.

9. **Use Secure Connections**  
   - Always use a secure, encrypted connection (VPN) when accessing corporate systems remotely.
   - Ensure that your computer and mobile devices are protected with a password or biometric authentication.

10. **Report Suspicious Activity**  
    - If you suspect that your account has been compromised, change your password immediately and notify the IT Help Desk.

## Compliance and Enforcement

11. **Policy Compliance**  
    - All employees must comply with this Password and Authentication Policy.
    - Non-compliance may result in disciplinary action, which could include termination.

12. **Review and Updates**  
    - This policy will be reviewed annually or as needed to address emerging security threats.
    - Employees will be notified of any changes to the policy.

For any questions regarding this policy, please contact the IT Help Desk at it-support@company.com.

title: Remote work IT policy
body:
# Remote Work IT Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the IT policies and procedures for employees working remotely to ensure data security, effective communication, and technology support.

### Policy Overview
All employees working remotely must adhere to the following guidelines to maintain productivity and security.

### Remote Work IT Guidelines

1. **Device Security**
   - Ensure your laptop and mobile devices are secured with a strong password.
   - Enable full disk encryption on all devices used for work.
   - Regularly update your operating system and software applications to the latest versions.

2. **Network Security**
   - Use a secure internet connection. Avoid public Wi-Fi networks whenever possible.
   - If using public Wi-Fi, connect through a Virtual Private Network (VPN) provided by the company.
   - Do not share your network password with anyone outside of your household.

3. **Data Protection**
   - Store all work-related files on the company’s cloud storage solution.
   - Avoid saving sensitive company data on personal devices or unsecured locations.
   - Ensure that sensitive information is shared only through secure channels, such as company-approved email or collaboration tools.

4. **Communication Tools**
   - Use company-approved communication tools (e.g., Microsoft Teams, Slack) for all work-related discussions.
   - Regularly check company communication channels for updates and announcements.
   - Schedule regular check-ins with your team to maintain collaboration and communication.

5. **IT Support**
   - For any technical issues, contact the IT support team via the helpdesk ticketing system.
   - Document all issues clearly, including screenshots if applicable, to facilitate quicker resolution.
   - Do not attempt unauthorized repairs or installations on company devices.

6. **Time Management**
   - Keep regular working hours as per your employment contract.
   - Use the company’s time-tracking tools to log your hours accurately.
   - Communicate any changes in your availability to your manager in advance.

7. **Compliance and Monitoring**
   - Adhere to all company policies regarding data privacy and security, including GDPR and other relevant regulations.
   - Be aware that remote work activities may be monitored for compliance with company policies.
   - Report any suspicious activities or security breaches to the IT department immediately.

8. **Home Office Setup**
   - Set up a dedicated workspace that minimizes distractions and promotes productivity.
   - Ensure that your workspace has adequate lighting and ergonomic furniture to support your health and well-being.
   - Keep your workspace organized and free of clutter to maintain focus.

### Conclusion
Following these guidelines helps to ensure a secure and productive remote work environment. For any questions or further clarification on the remote work IT policy, please contact the IT department.

title: Security incident reporting policy
body:
# Security Incident Reporting Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the procedures for reporting security incidents within our organization. Prompt reporting is crucial for effective incident response and minimizing potential damage.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors who have access to our information systems and data.

### Definition of a Security Incident
A security incident is any event that compromises the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information. Examples include unauthorized access, data breaches, malware infections, and physical security breaches.

### Reporting Steps

1. **Recognize the Incident**
   - Be aware of common indicators of a security incident, such as unusual system behavior, unauthorized access attempts, or unexpected data loss.

2. **Do Not Investigate Further**
   - Avoid attempting to investigate or resolve the issue yourself. This could worsen the situation or lead to data loss.

3. **Immediately Report the Incident**
   - Use the following channels to report the incident:
     - **Email**: Send a detailed email to the IT Security Team at security@company.com.
     - **Phone**: Call the IT Helpdesk at extension 1234.
     - **Incident Reporting Tool**: Log the incident in the company’s incident reporting software (link to software).

4. **Provide Detailed Information**
   - When reporting, include the following details:
     - Your name and contact information
     - Date and time of the incident
     - Description of the incident (what happened, where it occurred, how you noticed it)
     - Any immediate actions you took (e.g., disconnecting from the network)

5. **Preserve Evidence**
   - If safe to do so, document any evidence related to the incident. This may include screenshots, error messages, or logs. Do not modify or delete any files related to the incident.

6. **Follow Instructions from IT Security**
   - After reporting, follow any instructions provided by the IT Security Team. This may include isolating affected devices, changing passwords, or other remedial actions.

7. **Complete an Incident Report Form**
   - After the initial report, complete an Incident Report Form as requested by the IT Security Team. This form collects further details and helps in the analysis of the incident.

8. **Participate in the Investigation**
   - Be prepared to provide additional information or clarification as needed during the investigation. Your cooperation is essential for a thorough review.

9. **Review and Learn**
   - Once the incident is resolved, participate in any debriefing sessions conducted by the IT Security Team. This is crucial for understanding what happened and how to prevent similar incidents in the future.

### Conclusion
Timely reporting of security incidents is vital for protecting our organization’s information assets. Adhering to this policy helps ensure a swift response and mitigation of risks.

### Contact Information
For any questions regarding this policy, please contact the IT Security Team at security@company.com or call extension 1234.

title: Software approval and procurement policy
body:
# Software Approval and Procurement Policy

## Objective
To outline the steps required for the approval and procurement of software within the organization, ensuring compliance with budgetary, security, and operational standards.

## Scope
This policy applies to all employees seeking to acquire software for departmental or organizational use.

## Procedure

1. **Identify Software Needs**
   - Assess the specific business requirements for the software.
   - Consult with team members to gather input on features and functionalities.

2. **Research Available Options**
   - Conduct market research to identify potential software solutions.
   - Compare features, pricing, user reviews, and vendor reputation.

3. **Draft Software Proposal**
   - Create a proposal that includes:
     - Software name and description
     - Business needs addressed
     - Estimated costs (licensing, maintenance, support)
     - Expected benefits and ROI
     - Implementation timeline

4. **Obtain Managerial Approval**
   - Submit the proposal to your direct supervisor for initial review.
   - Address any feedback or concerns and revise the proposal as necessary.
   - Obtain written approval from your supervisor.

5. **Engage IT Department**
   - Forward the approved proposal to the IT department for a technical assessment.
   - IT will evaluate the software for:
     - Compatibility with existing systems
     - Security risks and compliance with IT policies
     - Support and maintenance requirements

6. **Vendor Evaluation**
   - If approved by IT, identify and evaluate vendors.
   - Request demonstrations or trials where possible.
   - Assess vendor support, training, and service level agreements (SLAs).

7. **Budget Review**
   - Submit the finalized proposal, including vendor details and cost estimates, to the finance department.
   - Ensure the purchase aligns with departmental budgets and financial policies.

8. **Final Approval**
   - Await final approval from the finance department.
   - Any purchase exceeding predetermined thresholds may require additional executive approval.

9. **Procurement Process**
   - Once approved, initiate the procurement process:
     - Create a purchase order (PO) through the procurement system.
     - Ensure compliance with any existing vendor contracts or agreements.
     - Maintain records of all communications and agreements with vendors.

10. **Implementation and Training**
    - Work with the vendor and IT to schedule implementation.
    - Arrange for necessary training sessions for end-users.
    - Document the software installation and setup process.

11. **Post-Implementation Review**
    - After implementation, conduct a review to ensure the software meets the intended business needs.
    - Gather feedback from users and report any issues to IT for resolution.

12. **Ongoing Evaluation**
    - Periodically assess the software's performance and relevance to ensure it continues to meet business needs.
    - Keep track of renewal dates and budget for future expenses.

## Compliance
All employees must adhere to this policy to ensure a consistent approach to software procurement, maintaining organizational standards and efficiency. Non-compliance may result in disciplinary action.

title: IT expense and approval thresholds
body:
# IT Expense and Approval Thresholds

## Purpose
This article outlines the expense thresholds for IT-related purchases and the approval process necessary to ensure compliance with company policy.

## Expense Thresholds
1. **Under $500**: 
   - No formal approval required.
   - Inform your manager of the purchase in advance.
   - Document the purchase in the expense tracking system.

2. **$500 - $2,500**:
   - Obtain email approval from your direct manager.
   - Fill out the IT Purchase Request Form (available on the intranet).
   - Submit the form along with any supporting documentation (quotes, product specs) to the IT finance team.

3. **$2,500 - $10,000**:
   - Require written approval from your manager and the IT department head.
   - Complete the IT Purchase Request Form and attach all necessary documentation.
   - Forward the request to the IT finance team for processing.

4. **Over $10,000**:
   - Mandatory approval from the IT department head and the CFO.
   - Fill out the IT Purchase Request Form with detailed justification, including cost-benefit analysis.
   - Submit the request to the IT finance team, who will present it during the monthly budget meeting for final approval.

## Steps for Requesting Approval
1. **Identify the Need**:
   - Clearly define the purpose of the purchase and how it aligns with departmental goals.

2. **Gather Information**:
   - Collect quotes, product specifications, and any other relevant information needed to support your request.

3. **Complete the IT Purchase Request Form**:
   - Ensure all fields are filled out accurately, including justification for the expense.

4. **Submit for Approval**:
   - Email your direct manager with the completed form and necessary documentation attached.
   - For amounts over $2,500, include the IT department head in the approval request.

5. **Track the Approval Process**:
   - Follow up with your manager and the IT finance team to ensure your request is being processed.
   - Keep a record of all communications regarding your request.

6. **Handle the Purchase**:
   - Once approved, proceed with the purchase as outlined in your request.
   - Retain all receipts and documentation for expense reporting.

## Important Notes
- All IT-related purchases must comply with the company’s procurement policies.
- Items purchased without proper approval may not be reimbursed.
- Regular audits will be conducted to ensure compliance with these thresholds.

For further questions or clarifications regarding IT expense approvals, please contact the IT finance team at it-finance@company.com.

title: Data retention policy
body:
# Data Retention Policy

## Purpose
This document outlines the data retention policy of [Company Name]. It establishes guidelines for managing data throughout its lifecycle to ensure compliance with legal requirements, protect sensitive information, and optimize storage resources.

## Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who handle data on behalf of [Company Name].

## Data Retention Guidelines

### 1. Identify Data Types
   - **Classify Data**: Categorize data into the following types:
     - Financial Records
     - Employee Records
     - Customer Information
     - Operational Documents
     - Email Communications
   - **Determine Sensitivity**: Assess the sensitivity of each data type to apply appropriate retention periods.

### 2. Establish Retention Periods
   - **Set Timeframes**: Retention periods should be based on legal requirements, industry standards, and business needs. Refer to the table below for guidance:

     | Data Type                | Retention Period                |
     |--------------------------|---------------------------------|
     | Financial Records        | 7 years                         |
     | Employee Records         | 7 years after termination       |
     | Customer Information      | 5 years after last interaction  |
     | Operational Documents    | 3 years                         |
     | Email Communications     | 2 years                         |

### 3. Implement Data Storage Solutions
   - **Choose Storage Solutions**: Ensure data is stored securely using approved platforms that comply with security standards.
   - **Backup Procedures**: Establish regular backup procedures for critical data, ensuring that backups align with retention periods.

### 4. Data Review Process
   - **Regular Audits**: Conduct bi-annual audits to review data storage and retention compliance.
   - **Identifying Redundant Data**: Use automated tools to identify and flag data that exceeds its retention period.

### 5. Secure Data Disposal
   - **Data Deletion Protocols**: Implement secure deletion processes for data that has reached its retention limit. This includes:
     - Permanent deletion of electronic files.
     - Shredding physical documents.
   - **Documentation**: Maintain records of deleted data for audit purposes.

### 6. Training and Communication
   - **Employee Training**: Conduct annual training sessions on data retention policies and procedures for all employees.
   - **Policy Accessibility**: Ensure the data retention policy is easily accessible on the company intranet.

### 7. Compliance and Violations
   - **Reporting Violations**: Employees must report any potential violations of this policy to their supervisor or the IT department immediately.
   - **Disciplinary Actions**: Non-compliance with this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination.

## Conclusion
Adhering to the data retention policy is essential for legal compliance and data integrity. Following these guidelines will help protect [Company Name] and its stakeholders while optimizing our data management practices. For any questions regarding this policy, please contact the IT department.

title: Device encryption policy
body:
# Device Encryption Policy

## Purpose
This document outlines the company’s device encryption policy to ensure the protection of sensitive data on all devices used for work purposes.

## Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who utilize company-owned or personal devices to access company data.

## Policy Statement
All devices accessing company data must be encrypted to safeguard sensitive information from unauthorized access in the event of loss or theft.

## Steps for Implementing Device Encryption

1. **Identify Devices**
   - List all devices that will be used for work-related tasks, including laptops, desktops, tablets, and smartphones.

2. **Check Device Compatibility**
   - Verify that the device's operating system supports encryption. Most modern operating systems, such as Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android, offer built-in encryption features.

3. **Backup Data**
   - Before initiating encryption, ensure that all important data is backed up. Use company-approved backup solutions to avoid potential data loss during the encryption process.

4. **Enable Device Encryption**
   - Follow the steps specific to your device's operating system:

   **For Windows:**
   - Go to **Settings** > **Update & Security** > **Device encryption**.
   - Toggle the device encryption option to "On."
   - If necessary, follow prompts to encrypt the drive.

   **For macOS:**
   - Open **System Preferences** > **Security & Privacy**.
   - Select the **FileVault** tab.
   - Click "Turn On FileVault" and follow the prompts.

   **For iOS:**
   - Go to **Settings** > **Face ID & Passcode** or **Touch ID & Passcode**.
   - Ensure that a passcode is set. Scroll down to **Data Protection** and ensure that encryption is enabled.

   **For Android:**
   - Go to **Settings** > **Security** > **Encryption & credentials**.
   - Select **Encrypt phone** and follow the on-screen instructions.

5. **Set Up Strong Passwords**
   - Ensure that all devices are protected with strong passwords or biometric authentication (e.g., fingerprint, facial recognition). Avoid easily guessable passwords.

6. **Regularly Update Software**
   - Keep the device operating system and applications updated to the latest versions. Regular updates often include security patches that enhance encryption protocols.

7. **Educate Employees**
   - Conduct training sessions to inform employees about the importance of device encryption, how to verify that their devices are encrypted, and best practices for maintaining device security.

8. **Monitor Compliance**
   - IT will regularly check devices for compliance with the encryption policy. Non-compliant devices may be restricted from accessing company data until encryption is applied.

9. **Report Issues**
   - If you encounter any issues with device encryption, report them to the IT department immediately for assistance.

## Conclusion
Adhering to this device encryption policy is essential for protecting sensitive company data. Compliance will be monitored, and failure to adhere to this policy may result in disciplinary action. For further information, please contact the IT department.

title: Approved SaaS and shadow-IT policy
body:
# Approved SaaS and Shadow-IT Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the guidelines for using Software as a Service (SaaS) applications within our organization and addresses the management of shadow IT. By adhering to this policy, employees can ensure compliance with security standards and maintain operational efficiency.

### Policy Guidelines

1. **Approved SaaS Applications**
   - Employees are required to use only the SaaS applications listed in the approved software catalog. This list is maintained by the IT department and can be found on the company intranet.
   - Regular updates to the approved list will be communicated through company-wide emails and posted on the intranet.

2. **Requesting New SaaS Applications**
   - If a specific SaaS application is not on the approved list, employees can submit a request for evaluation.
   - To request a new application, follow these steps:
     1. Fill out the SaaS Request Form available on the IT intranet.
     2. Provide a detailed justification for the request, including the application’s purpose, expected benefits, and any relevant compliance considerations.
     3. Submit the form to your department manager for initial approval.
     4. The department manager will forward the request to the IT department for further evaluation.

3. **Shadow IT Management**
   - Shadow IT refers to the use of unauthorized SaaS applications by employees without IT knowledge or approval. To mitigate risks associated with shadow IT:
     - Employees must refrain from using any unapproved applications for work-related tasks.
     - If you discover that you or a colleague are using unapproved SaaS applications, report it to the IT department immediately for assessment.

4. **Security and Compliance**
   - All approved SaaS applications must comply with the organization’s security and data protection policies. This includes, but is not limited to:
     - Data encryption (in transit and at rest)
     - Regular security audits
     - User access controls
   - The IT department will conduct annual reviews of all approved applications to ensure ongoing compliance.

5. **Employee Training and Awareness**
   - All employees will receive training on the approved SaaS and shadow IT policy during onboarding. Refresher training sessions will be held annually.
   - Employees are encouraged to stay informed about the approved applications and any updates to the policy by regularly checking the intranet.

6. **Non-compliance Consequences**
   - Non-compliance with this policy may result in disciplinary action, including removal of access to company resources and potential termination.
   - Employees found using unapproved SaaS applications may also face potential data security risks, including data breaches and loss of sensitive information.

### Conclusion
Adhering to the approved SaaS and shadow IT policy is essential for maintaining the integrity, security, and efficiency of our IT systems. For questions or further clarification regarding this policy, please contact the IT department at it-support@company.com.

title: IT offboarding policy
body:
# IT Offboarding Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the standard operating procedures for the offboarding process of employees within the IT department. Proper offboarding ensures the security of company data and a smooth transition for both the departing employee and the team.

### Procedure

1. **Notification of Departure**
   - The manager must notify the IT department at least two weeks prior to the employee’s last working day.
   - HR will send an official resignation letter or termination notice to IT.

2. **Access Review**
   - The IT department will conduct an access review of all systems, applications, and data the employee has access to.
   - Document all systems where access needs to be revoked.

3. **Revoke Access**
   - On the employee's last working day, revoke access to all company systems, including:
     - Email accounts
     - Network access (VPN, Wi-Fi, etc.)
     - Internal applications (CRM, ERP, etc.)
     - Third-party services (e.g., cloud storage, SaaS applications)
   - Change passwords for shared accounts the employee had access to.

4. **Data Retrieval**
   - Schedule a meeting with the departing employee to retrieve any company-owned devices, including:
     - Laptops
     - Mobile phones
     - External drives
   - Ensure that all company data is backed up and transferred to the appropriate team members. This includes files, documents, and ongoing projects.

5. **Conduct Exit Interview**
   - IT management should conduct an exit interview focusing on the employee's experience with IT services.
   - Gather feedback on system performance, any unresolved technical issues, and recommendations for improvements.

6. **Update Documentation**
   - Update internal documentation to reflect the employee's departure, including:
     - Organizational charts
     - Project management tools
     - Team directories

7. **Final Checklist**
   - Use the IT Offboarding Checklist to ensure all steps are completed. The checklist should include:
     - Access revocation confirmation
     - Equipment return confirmation
     - Data retrieval completion
     - Exit interview notes

8. **Notify Stakeholders**
   - Inform relevant stakeholders (HR, direct team members, project managers) about the employee’s departure and any necessary transition plans.
   - Assign temporary responsibilities to team members as needed.

9. **Post-Offboarding Review**
   - Schedule a follow-up meeting within one month to review the offboarding process and identify any areas for improvement.
   - Ensure all documentation related to the offboarding process is archived for future reference.

### Conclusion
Following this IT offboarding policy ensures that company data remains secure and the transition is seamless for both the departing employee and the team. Adherence to these steps will help maintain operational integrity and protect sensitive information.

title: Travel IT and international access policy
body:
# Travel IT and International Access Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This article outlines the procedures and guidelines for accessing corporate IT resources while traveling internationally. Adhering to these guidelines will ensure the security of corporate data and compliance with international regulations.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees who will be traveling outside of their home country for business purposes.

### Guidelines

1. **Pre-Travel Preparation**
   - **Notify IT Department**: Inform the IT department at least two weeks prior to your travel. Provide details about your destination and duration of stay.
   - **Device Security Assessment**: Ensure your devices (laptops, tablets, smartphones) are compliant with corporate security standards. IT will assist in conducting a security assessment.
   - **Install VPN**: Confirm that you have the corporate VPN installed and configured on your devices. IT will provide instructions if needed.

2. **Data Backup**
   - **Backup Important Data**: Before traveling, back up all important data to the corporate cloud storage or an external hard drive. This ensures that no data is lost in case of device theft or malfunction.
   - **Limit Local Data Storage**: Avoid storing sensitive data locally on your devices. Access necessary files directly from the corporate cloud when possible.

3. **Traveling with Devices**
   - **Use Company-Issued Devices**: Whenever possible, use company-issued devices for travel. Personal devices can pose additional security risks.
   - **Physical Security**: Keep your devices secure at all times. Use a lock for your laptop in hotel rooms and do not leave devices unattended in public places.

4. **Network Access**
   - **Avoid Public Wi-Fi**: Refrain from using public Wi-Fi networks, as they may not be secure. If necessary, use a personal hotspot or a secure VPN connection.
   - **Connect to Corporate VPN**: Always connect to the corporate VPN when accessing sensitive corporate data. Ensure that the VPN is active before logging into any corporate services.

5. **Compliance with Local Laws**
   - **Research Local Regulations**: Be aware of local laws and regulations regarding data access and privacy. Some countries may impose restrictions on VPN usage or data transfer.
   - **Respect Local Customs**: Be mindful of local customs and practices regarding technology and data handling.

6. **Post-Travel Procedures**
   - **Device Inspection**: Upon returning, have your devices inspected by IT for any potential security breaches or malware. This should be done within 48 hours of your return.
   - **Change Passwords**: Change your corporate passwords immediately after returning from your trip to enhance security.

7. **Reporting Incidents**
   - **Report Security Incidents**: If you suspect any security breaches or if your devices are lost or stolen, report the incident to IT immediately.

### Conclusion
Following this travel IT and international access policy ensures the security of corporate information and compliance with regulations while traveling abroad. For any questions or assistance, please contact the IT department.

title: Printing and sustainability policy
body:
# Printing and Sustainability Policy

## Category: Other

### Objective
To provide guidelines for responsible printing practices that promote sustainability and reduce the environmental impact of our operations.

### Policy Guidelines

1. **Assess the Need for Printing**
   - Before printing, ask yourself if the document needs to be printed. Consider whether it can be shared electronically via email or cloud storage.

2. **Use Digital Formats**
   - Utilize digital formats for documentation, presentations, and collaboration. Tools such as Google Docs, Microsoft Teams, and SharePoint should be the first choice for sharing information.

3. **Double-Sided Printing**
   - Always set your printer to double-sided by default. This reduces paper usage by up to 50%. 
   - When printing, select “Print on Both Sides” in the print settings.

4. **Print in Black and White**
   - Default to black and white printing unless color is essential for understanding the content. This saves on ink and reduces costs.

5. **Preview Before Printing**
   - Use the print preview function to check the document layout and reduce unnecessary pages. This helps to minimize mistakes, which can lead to wasted prints.

6. **Use Recycled Paper**
   - When printing is necessary, opt for recycled paper. Our office supplies store offers various options, so check availability.

7. **Limit Print Quantity**
   - Only print the number of copies you truly need. For meetings or presentations, consider whether handouts are necessary or if participants can view materials on their devices.

8. **Collect and Recycle Used Paper**
   - Set up designated bins in the office for used paper. Ensure that all employees are aware of and utilize these bins for recycling. 

9. **Monitor Printing Practices**
   - Regularly review printing habits as a team. Use available software to track individual and departmental printing volumes, and encourage discussions around reducing usage.

10. **Educate and Engage Employees**
    - Conduct quarterly training sessions to highlight the importance of sustainability in printing. Encourage employees to share tips and best practices.

11. **Implement Print Quotas**
    - Consider setting print quotas for departments based on their needs and past usage. This encourages responsible printing and awareness of consumption.

12. **Feedback Loop**
    - Create a feedback mechanism for employees to suggest improvements to the printing and sustainability policy. Regularly review and update the policy based on the feedback received.

### Conclusion
Sustainability is a shared responsibility. By following these guidelines, we can collectively minimize our environmental impact and contribute to a more sustainable workplace. Let’s work together to create a culture of responsible printing. 

For questions or further information on this policy, please contact the IT department.

title: Mobile device management policy
body:
# Mobile Device Management Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
To establish guidelines for the use, security, and management of mobile devices within the organization to protect corporate data and maintain compliance with industry standards.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors who use mobile devices to access corporate resources.

### Policy Guidelines

1. **Device Enrollment**
   - All mobile devices must be enrolled in the company’s Mobile Device Management (MDM) system prior to accessing corporate resources.
   - Employees should request enrollment through the IT department by submitting a Mobile Device Enrollment Request Form.

2. **Supported Devices**
   - Only company-approved devices (smartphones, tablets) are permitted for access to corporate data. A list of approved devices is available on the IT intranet page.
   - Personal devices may not be used for accessing corporate resources unless explicitly authorized.

3. **Security Requirements**
   - All devices must have a secure passcode or biometric authentication enabled.
   - Devices must have the latest operating system and security patches installed.
   - Employees are responsible for ensuring that security settings are maintained in accordance with company guidelines.

4. **Data Protection**
   - Sensitive corporate data must be stored in secure applications as designated by IT (e.g., corporate email, file-sharing apps).
   - Users must not store sensitive data locally on their devices.
   - Remote wipe capabilities will be enabled on all devices to ensure data can be erased if the device is lost or stolen.

5. **Usage Guidelines**
   - Mobile devices must be used primarily for work-related purposes.
   - Personal use should be limited and should not interfere with work responsibilities.
   - Employees should avoid connecting to unsecured public Wi-Fi networks when accessing corporate resources.

6. **Reporting Lost or Stolen Devices**
   - Employees must report any lost or stolen devices to the IT department immediately.
   - A report should be submitted using the Lost or Stolen Device Report Form, which will initiate the remote wipe process if necessary.

7. **Compliance and Monitoring**
   - The IT department will regularly monitor device compliance with this policy.
   - Non-compliance may result in disciplinary action, including revocation of access to corporate resources.

8. **Policy Review**
   - This policy will be reviewed annually and updated as necessary to ensure compliance with industry standards and best practices.

### Additional Resources
- [Mobile Device Enrollment Request Form](#)
- [Lost or Stolen Device Report Form](#)
- [IT Intranet Page for Approved Devices](#)

### Contact Information
For any questions regarding this policy or assistance with mobile device management, please contact the IT Helpdesk at it-support@company.com or call extension 1234.

title: Hardware refresh policy (2022, 4-year cycle)
body:
# Hardware Refresh Policy (2022, 4-Year Cycle)

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the hardware refresh policy for the organization, ensuring that all employees have access to up-to-date technology to enhance productivity and efficiency.

### Policy Overview
In 2022, the organization adopted a 4-year hardware refresh cycle for all desktop and laptop computers. This policy aims to standardize hardware procurement and replace outdated equipment, thus minimizing downtime and maintaining optimal performance.

### Scope
This policy applies to all full-time employees who are provided with company-owned desktop or laptop computers.

### Steps for Hardware Refresh

1. **Eligibility**  
   - All desktop and laptop computers are eligible for refresh every 4 years from the date of acquisition. Hardware purchased after January 1, 2022, will be eligible for refresh in January 2026.

2. **Assessment Period**  
   - An assessment will be conducted annually to identify which devices are due for refresh. The IT department will compile a list based on purchase dates and performance metrics.

3. **Notification**  
   - Employees with devices identified for refresh will be notified via email at least 3 months before the scheduled refresh. This notification will include information about the replacement process and timeline.

4. **Device Collection**  
   - A week prior to the scheduled refresh date, IT will arrange for the collection of old devices. Employees must ensure that all personal files are backed up and that devices are returned in good condition. 

5. **Data Migration**  
   - IT will provide guidance on how to back up personal data. Employees are responsible for initiating the backup process. IT will assist in transferring data to the new device upon request.

6. **New Device Deployment**  
   - New devices will be configured and deployed within one week of collection of the old devices. Employees will receive an email with instructions on how to access their new hardware and set it up.

7. **Training and Support**  
   - IT will offer training sessions on new hardware and software features as needed. Employees can also reach out to the IT helpdesk for ongoing support.

8. **Compliance**  
   - Employees are expected to comply with the hardware refresh policy. Failure to return old devices may result in disciplinary action and/or financial liability for the employee.

9. **Exceptions**  
   - Exceptions to the refresh cycle may be considered for specific roles that require specialized equipment. Requests for exceptions must be submitted in writing to the IT Manager for review and approval.

10. **Review of Policy**  
    - This policy will be reviewed annually and updated as necessary to reflect technological advancements and organizational needs.

### Conclusion
Adhering to the hardware refresh policy ensures that all employees have access to the necessary tools to perform their jobs effectively. For any questions regarding this policy, please contact the IT department.

title: Remote access / VPN usage policy
body:
# Remote Access / VPN Usage Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This document outlines the policy for remote access and VPN usage to ensure the security and integrity of the company's data while enabling employees to work efficiently from remote locations.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party users who require remote access to the company’s network.

### Policy Statement
Employees must use the company-provided Virtual Private Network (VPN) service to access the corporate network remotely. This ensures secure connections and protects sensitive data from unauthorized access.

### Guidelines for Remote Access and VPN Usage

1. **Eligibility for Remote Access**
   - Employees must obtain prior approval from their direct supervisor and the IT department to access the corporate network remotely.

2. **VPN Installation**
   - Download the company VPN client from the corporate intranet.
   - Follow the installation instructions provided in the “VPN Client Installation Guide” located on the intranet.

3. **Authentication**
   - Employees must use their corporate credentials to log in to the VPN.
   - Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) if prompted during the login process, as this adds an additional layer of security.

4. **Connection Procedures**
   - Open the VPN client on your device.
   - Select the appropriate server location as per your work requirements.
   - Click “Connect” and enter your credentials if prompted.

5. **Data Security**
   - Ensure that your device has up-to-date antivirus and anti-malware software installed.
   - Do not access sensitive data over public Wi-Fi networks unless connected to the VPN.
   - Always log out of the VPN when not in use to minimize security risks.

6. **Acceptable Use**
   - Remote access should be used for company-related business only. Personal use is strictly prohibited.
   - Employees must adhere to the company's Code of Conduct while working remotely.

7. **Reporting Issues**
   - If you experience any issues connecting to the VPN, contact the IT helpdesk at [IT support email/phone number].
   - Report any suspicious activity immediately to the IT department.

8. **Compliance**
   - Regular audits will be conducted to ensure compliance with this policy.
   - Non-compliance may result in disciplinary action, including revocation of remote access privileges.

### Conclusion
By following this Remote Access / VPN Usage Policy, employees can ensure a secure and efficient work environment while accessing the corporate network remotely. Always prioritize security and report any issues promptly.

title: Acceptable use of AI tools policy
body:
# Acceptable Use of AI Tools Policy

## Category: Other

### Purpose
This policy outlines the acceptable use of AI tools within our organization to ensure compliance with legal standards, protect sensitive data, and promote ethical use of technology.

### Scope
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors who utilize AI tools in their work.

### Policy Guidelines

1. **Authorization**
   - Ensure that the use of AI tools is pre-approved by your manager or the IT department.
   - Only use AI tools that are vetted and sanctioned by our organization.

2. **Data Privacy**
   - Do not input sensitive or confidential information into AI tools unless explicitly authorized.
   - Familiarize yourself with the data handling and privacy policies associated with the AI tools you are using.

3. **Compliance with Regulations**
   - Adhere to all relevant laws and regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) when utilizing AI technologies.
   - Consult the legal department if you are unsure about compliance requirements.

4. **Ethical Use**
   - Use AI tools responsibly and avoid generating or disseminating false or misleading information.
   - Ensure that AI-generated content is reviewed for accuracy and appropriateness before sharing.

5. **Intellectual Property**
   - Respect intellectual property rights when using AI tools, including copyright and trademark laws.
   - Do not use proprietary data or algorithms from third parties without proper licensing.

6. **Security Measures**
   - Keep all software and applications, including AI tools, updated to the latest versions.
   - Report any security breaches or suspicious activity related to AI usage to the IT department immediately.

7. **Training and Awareness**
   - Participate in training sessions on the responsible use of AI tools as required by the organization.
   - Stay informed about updates to the policy and any new AI tools introduced within the company.

8. **Monitoring and Enforcement**
   - Understand that the organization reserves the right to monitor the use of AI tools to ensure compliance with this policy.
   - Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination.

### Conclusion
By following this policy, we can leverage the benefits of AI tools while mitigating risks and ensuring a safe, ethical work environment. For any questions or clarifications regarding this policy, please contact the IT department.

title: AstraCore v4.8 release notes
body:
# AstraCore v4.8 Release Notes

**Category:** Software  
**Product Version:** v4.8  
**Release Date:** [Insert Release Date]

## New Features and Enhancements

1. **Enhanced User Interface**
   - Redesigned dashboard for better navigation and usability.
   - New theme options for improved accessibility.

2. **Performance Improvements**
   - Optimized data processing algorithms resulting in a 30% faster response time.
   - Reduced memory usage by 20% during peak loads.

3. **API Integration**
   - New RESTful API endpoints for easier integration with third-party applications.
   - Enhanced security features for API access, including OAuth 2.0 support.

4. **Reporting Module**
   - Added customizable reporting templates.
   - Improved data visualization options, including new graph types.

5. **Bug Fixes**
   - Resolved issue where users experienced crashes during data export.
   - Fixed a bug that caused incorrect calculations in the financial module.

## Upgrade Instructions

### Pre-Upgrade Checklist

1. **Backup Data**
   - Ensure you have a complete backup of your current AstraCore database.
   - Verify backup integrity by performing a test restore.

2. **Review System Requirements**
   - Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for AstraCore v4.8:
     - Operating System: Windows Server 2019 or later, Linux (latest LTS version)
     - RAM: Minimum 8GB (16GB recommended)
     - Disk Space: Minimum 20GB available

3. **Notify Users**
   - Inform all users of the scheduled upgrade and expected downtime.

### Upgrade Steps

1. **Download the Upgrade Package**
   - Access the internal software repository and download the AstraCore v4.8 upgrade package.

2. **Stop AstraCore Services**
   - Navigate to the services panel on your server:
     - **Windows:** Open Services (services.msc), find AstraCore services, and select Stop.
     - **Linux:** Run `sudo systemctl stop astracore`.

3. **Install the Upgrade**
   - Extract the downloaded package.
   - Run the installation script:
     - **Windows:** Double-click `setup.exe` and follow the on-screen instructions.
     - **Linux:** Open a terminal and execute `sudo ./install.sh`.

4. **Run Database Migrations**
   - After installation, run the migration script to update the database schema:
     - Using command line, execute `./migrate.sh`.

5. **Restart Services**
   - Start AstraCore services again:
     - **Windows:** Go back to Services and select Start for AstraCore services.
     - **Linux:** Run `sudo systemctl start astracore`.

6. **Verify Installation**
   - Log in to AstraCore and check the version number in the footer to ensure it reads v4.8.
   - Test key functionalities to confirm successful upgrade.

## Post-Upgrade Actions

1. **User Training**
   - Schedule training sessions for users to familiarize them with new features.

2. **Monitor Performance**
   - Monitor system performance and user feedback for any issues post-upgrade.

3. **Update Documentation**
   - Ensure all internal documentation reflects changes made in v4.8.

For support or questions regarding the upgrade process, please contact the IT support team.

title: AstraCore v4.9 release notes
body:
# AstraCore v4.9 Release Notes

**Category:** Software  
**Product Version:** v4.9  
**Release Date:** [Insert Release Date]

## New Features and Enhancements

1. **Enhanced User Interface**
   - Redesigned dashboard for better navigation and usability.
   - New theme options for improved accessibility.

2. **Performance Improvements**
   - Optimized data processing algorithms resulting in a 30% faster response time.
   - Reduced memory usage by 20% during peak loads.

3. **API Updates**
   - Introduced new endpoints for integration with third-party applications.
   - Deprecated older API endpoints; refer to the API documentation for migration steps.

4. **Security Enhancements**
   - Implemented multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user accounts.
   - Enhanced encryption protocols for data at rest and in transit.

5. **Bug Fixes**
   - Resolved issues related to session timeouts.
   - Fixed a bug causing incorrect data display in reports.

## Upgrade Instructions

### Prerequisites
- Ensure you have administrative access to the AstraCore environment.
- Backup your current version of AstraCore including databases and configuration files.

### Step-by-Step Upgrade Process

1. **Download the Upgrade Package**
   - Access the internal software repository and download the AstraCore v4.9 upgrade package.

2. **Review Release Notes**
   - Familiarize yourself with the changes in this release and any deprecated features.

3. **Backup Existing Data**
   - Execute the following command to backup your database:
     ```bash
     mysqldump -u [username] -p [database_name] > backup_v4.8.sql
     ```
   - Also, ensure that all configuration files are saved.

4. **Stop Current Services**
   - Stop the AstraCore application services to prevent data inconsistencies:
     ```bash
     sudo systemctl stop astracore
     ```

5. **Install the Upgrade**
   - Navigate to the directory where the upgrade package is located and run:
     ```bash
     sudo tar -xvf astracore_v4.9.tar.gz -C /opt/astracore
     ```

6. **Migrate the Database**
   - Run the migration script to update the database schema:
     ```bash
     cd /opt/astracore/scripts
     sudo ./migrate_db.sh
     ```

7. **Start Services**
   - Restart the AstraCore application services:
     ```bash
     sudo systemctl start astracore
     ```

8. **Verify the Upgrade**
   - Log in to the application and confirm that you are running version 4.9.
   - Check system logs for any errors:
     ```bash
     tail -f /var/log/astracore.log
     ```

## Known Issues

- **UI Rendering**: Some users may experience rendering issues in Internet Explorer. It is recommended to use modern browsers like Chrome or Firefox.
- **API Rate Limiting**: Users might encounter rate limiting on new API endpoints. Please refer to the documentation for best practices.

## Support

For further assistance, please contact the IT support team at [support@company.com] or visit the internal support portal. 

**End of Document**
